summary: The formal_conjectures dataset contains 4,557 problems with 2,571 Lean 4 conjectures, of which 1,029 remain open (marked 'sorry'). Activity data is sparse and partly inconsistent: quarterly buckets (q1-q10) each show a uniform 100 issues, while the only per-problem record (fc0000) shows a single resolved item, suggesting tracking is in an early or aggregate-only state.
problems: fc0000: 1 resolved issue ('Proof: getCategoryStatsMarkdown'), 0 open — appears to be tooling/reporting rather than a math conjecture
q1-q10 each report a flat 100 issues, an unrealistically uniform distribution that likely reflects pagination caps or a default limit rather than true counts
No granular per-conjecture issue data is available for the 1,029 open ('sorry') problems, making prioritization difficult
Domain breakdown (number theory, combinatorics, analysis, algebra) is described but not mapped to issue activity
highlights: Large, well-scoped corpus: 2,571 formalized conjectures with clear open/closed status via 'sorry' markers
1,029 open problems provide a substantial, machine-checkable target set for automated and assisted proving
fc0000 demonstrates the resolution pipeline works end-to-end (issue opened and resolved)
Even quarterly distribution of issues indicates broad, sustained engagement across the dataset

suggested_todos.title: Fix issue-count instrumentation: the uniform 100-per-quarter figures indicate a query/pagination cap masking real activity
Generate a per-problem open-issue index for the 1,029 'sorry' conjectures to enable real traction ranking
Classify open conjectures by domain (number theory, combinatorics, analysis, algebra) to target high-yield areas
Identify low-complexity 'sorry' lemmas as quick-win candidates likely solvable within the week
Separate tooling/reporting items (e.g., fc0000 getCategoryStatsMarkdown) from mathematical conjectures in the tracker
